summary: Stored Dataset Viewer and DFM last-used preferences per project and Windows user.
details: Dataset Viewer, DFM, and future pages now share one project-specific last-used Reserving Class value named lastReservingClassPath under projects/<project>/users/<windows-login>/preferences.json, while Dataset Viewer and DFM keep only their page-specific fields in datasetViewer and dfmObject.
New standalone DFM tabs now restore the last DFM Details defaults from dfmObject, including the last DFM Name, after the project-user preferences load.

Dataset picker preferences, reserving-class tree filter specs/preferences, and hidden reserving-class paths are also stored in the project-user preference file so they copy with project duplication. The old AppData reserving_class_filter_spec.json file is no longer read or written.
